Just now

--

A subtle prompting mistake that was holding me back
Early on, I’d ask things like “What should I write about?” or “What should I do next?” The answers were… fine. Generic, safe, not particularly useful.
Then I changed my approach. Instead of asking AI to decide for me, I started giving it options and asking it to help me evaluate: “Here are three topics I’m considering: [list].
Which one would likely interest beginners most, and why?”
The difference was huge. Instead of vague suggestions, I got reasoning I could actually use and disagree with if needed.
The lesson: AI is better as a thinking partner than a decision-maker. Bring it your half-formed ideas, not a blank request for ideas. You’ll get sharper, more useful responses – and you stay in the driver’s seat.
